input_dict.question: The musician also known as "Braddah Iz"  is a member of what Hawaiian musical group?
input_dict.source_summary: Israel Kamakawiwoʻole ("Braddah Iz") was a member and founding member of the Hawaiian group The Mākaha Sons of Niʻihau (later known simply as The Makaha Sons).
input_dict.target_summary: Israel Kaʻanoʻi Kamakawiwoʻole (May 20, 1959 – June 26, 1997), also called "Braddah Iz", was a Native Hawaiian musician. The second-hop document 'Mākaha Sons' states: "The Mākaha Sons of Ni'ihau are a Hawaiian musical group formed in 1976 on the island of Oahu by Jerome Koko, Louis \"Moon\" Kauakahi, Skippy Kamakawiwo'ole, Israel Kamakawiwoʻole, and Sam Gray." Integrating these sources: the evidence shows Israel Kamakawiwoʻole (aka Braddah Iz) was among the founding members of The Mākaha Sons of Ni'ihau (the Hawaiian musical group referenced).
input_dict.candidate_summary: The passages contradict the context's claim that none of the provided passages name his Hawaiian group: they identify him as a member/founding member of The Mākaha Sons of Niʻihau. Passage [0] names Israel Kamakawiwoʻole among the founders of The Mākaha Sons of Ni'ihau.
